rag_rules: Fix unresolved references to time constant values by declaring them as visible constants in the component type annex before using them in assume/guarantee expressions.
Ensure that all AGREE expressions used in assume and guarantee statements are of the correct type (bool, int, or real) to avoid type mismatch errors.
Use proper AGREE syntax for logical expressions and temporal operators, ensuring that branching logic is nested correctly and that all identifiers are properly declared.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, always ensure that referenced identifiers are declared in the same annex or are visible features of the component.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts.
Place component type contracts in the appropriate annex scope, ensuring that assumptions and guarantees refer to visible ports and features only.
Validate that all AGREE expressions resolve to valid types (bool, int, real) to prevent syntax and type errors during validation.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with explicit types: const name : type = value; eq name : type = expression;
Use logical operators like `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` for expressing complex conditions in AGREE.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing undeclared identifiers or using invalid types in AGREE expressions, as this leads to unresolved reference and type mismatch errors.
Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes; use implementation scopes for such declarations.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
